rationale: Registry maps logical collection IDs to file/lock paths; NewStore resolves through DefaultRegistry preserving .ddx/beads.jsonl semantics. Only 'beads' is registered, deferring archive collections to C3. Fallback keeps existing call sites working.

findings.summary: Registry plays the 'owns named collections' role rather than a Collection/Store type per se; Store still owns IO and now resolves paths via the registry. Reasonable interpretation of AC1.
Tests not re-run in this review session; relying on the execute-bead pipeline's success signal (result.json outcome=task_succeeded) for AC3/AC4.
